Remember, marketing and sales are very, very different things. The idea is, as I always say, the secret to success in any business, doesn't matter what you do. You want to have an awesome kick-ass marketing campaign. How do you bring people into your sales funnel? That's a huge thing. Massively important. But there's a certain point when marketing ends and sales begins. What do you do when you get that person on the phone? Well, you're going to find certain things as we learned in the last module in prospecting. What did we learn? We learned that basically when someone comes in your funnel, there's four types of buyers. We'll say there's four types of people, two of which are buyers, two of which are not buyers. We have your buyers in heat and power. These are your potentially good buyers. Those who need, want your product, can benefit from your product and are qualified. It's a very basic concept in sales. Very basic. Then you have those ones that are in heat, meaning there's urgency. They want to buy now. They're feeling pain. It's in the forefront of their mind to make a decision. Then you have those buyers in power, which is the ones that need one and could use your product and could afford it. But they're feeling that they are okay. The house isn't on fire, so to speak. So they're not as urgent, still great buyers, still very much closeable, but that lack of urgency makes them feel like they're in the driver's seat. They're in a position of power. Hence the term buyers in power. For those of you on certification, you know I taught you guys strategy. Again, you're going to actually be revisiting that same strategy now next week, which is script building, which is unbelievable, by the way. You'll be getting that in a few days, the script building module. I'm still adding to it every day because I never stop with this stuff. In terms of how do you take a buyer in power and through language and through tonality and body language for that matter, if you're a person, turn that person, elevate that person, and they become a buyer in heat. As I always say about the straight line system, right? It's a backwards looking system. I mean, why does it work so well? Well, there's a lot of reasons, but one thing is, it's caused that moment of clarity that I had back in the late 80s was like, you know, okay, I'm doing all this stuff automatically without thinking about it. I out close everyone around me by a country mile. It was ridiculous. Well, what's happening is I turned around and said to myself, well, okay, what has to line up in my mind? I was doing, what would I need to say? What would the prospect have to hear in order to feel comfortable and confident enough to say yes? What is it backwards being not like, okay, I want to close the guy. That's looking forward. I'm here at the start. I want to close the guy. That's where I'm going, right? But how about a shift in perspective? Imagine I just close the sale, close the deal, achieve some goal in my life, right? You know, accomplish something, right? Well, imagine this. I'm saying, I've got to do that. How about this? The guy just bought what happened, what lined up, what were the things that transpired, why wouldn't he buy in the beginning? What made him buy again? What did I have to line up? You look backwards. Well, I began to realize, well, wait a second. If I, when I, when I got someone to say yes in the past, every time I done that, it was because these same key elements got lined up somehow. Those somehow, some way I was able to get somewhere along these three close, the two more, I was lining them up by looking backwards. See, I knew where I wanted to go. And by being there, I thought, here's my, okay, here's my starting point. It's like vision in reverse, essentially, right? Think of it that way. It's like, here's my vision. Okay. I want, I'm starting from my end point. Here I am. Here I am in the future when I got what I want five years down the road, where my life is exactly as I want it to be. There's nothing I'm pretty sure. All right. Okay. Why I was able to accomplish all that. Step number one, step number one, ready for this? I possessed. I'm going to tell you, this is a really interesting thing. I worked on this in a very different way, right? I possessed. Boutloads of what we call self-belief. In other words, in my deepest core, my crux of who Jordan Belfort is, how Jordan Belfort views himself, what his identity is. I was still a winner in my deepest core. Core. I absolutely had no doubt that I possessed every skill set. Ready for this? Every skill set. I possessed them. Every strategy, every tactic. I possessed every single one I would ever potentially need to achieve that outcome. That's not true. What did I leave out? And here's the big one. I said that on purpose. I'll repeat it. I, Jordan Belfort possessed every possible tactic, strategy, skill, connection, relationship, ability, every, I had it all. Who's that guy? I knew everything one would need to know to get where I wanted to go. And there's something missing from that equation. Want to know what it is? I'll tell you. Step number two, as part of this self-belief, I knew that I could go out there and learn or attract into my life anything that was missing. I wasn't delusional to think I really knew everything I would need to know, but I knew for sure that whatever I didn't know, whatever I needed to learn, whoever I needed to attract, whatever had to happen, that I would figure it out, learn it, do it, accomplish it. Along the way, I had no doubt, no seeds of doubt, not even a shred of doubt, that there was nothing that I could have possibly learned. And those first two steps, my friends, is the perfect storm for living an empowered life. It all starts there. You have the vision, but do you really believe that? Are you capable of it? Do you already possess what you need to possess and footnote number two and whatever you don't possess, you know you can learn. Or do you really, at your deepest, most honest self, say, you know, I'm really not that guy. I couldn't imagine myself being played by Leonor de Caprio or standing on a stage talking to 10,000 people, which I now do in my sleep. I do it so often. I don't believe I could run a billion dollar company. I don't believe I can come back from failure and lead people all over. I don't believe I don't see myself as a beacon in light. I don't know enough about internet marketing right now. Yeah, I'm a great salesperson, but I don't know about marketing. I don't, you know, this whole coding thing, you know, technology, I was born too early. I missed the boat in that. I don't know how to code. I would eat the code. Or do you think that I can hire the best because my vision will be so inspiring that everyone's going to want to jump on board to make my vision a reality. And that now, my friend, takes you to step three is do you have the ability to share this vision in a way that connects with people? Inspires them and moves them to take action to help you accomplish it. Do you know how to share it? Do you really know? Could you look a top level programmer coder in the eye, explain what you want to accomplish and just self say, wow, could you sit in front of a venture capitalist and talk about with passion and conviction? And the authority financially and a deep tacit understanding of what you really do for a living meaning is so going to give you a million bucks. It's so little pot with their hard earned money or harder money they manage other people. Is that you? Or is that not you? You could have run into that situation on the way up to the top. And let me tell you something and you're want to be a top producer here. You want to be a number one guy in a company be valued and move up the chain, get big commission checks. And I slowly but surely through this process of pivoting, working, pivoting, and on the pivoting, I end up moving towards my vision. Pretty logical, right? Now the danger here, and before we start the Q and A, what I really want to read for us here is that what you're going to see in the real world, in real business, in real life, in real sales, that if you have what's called tunnel vision, two eyes, firmly focus, lock on your vision, do whatever it takes to rescue for failure and happiness, rigidity, not going to live a very happy and power life. We must have what's called bifurcated focus, meaning one eye, by split, one eye, focus firmly on your vision with laser guided precision, while the other eye scans the horizon. It's like, whoop, whoop. It's like a radar scanning the horizon for opportunities that come up along the way on the way to your vision. And then you integrate those ones that make sense into your vision. Your vision is a living, breathing, evolving organism. But here's the really the rub when you get down to it. How would you know what might make sense to focus on? How would you know if it should be integrated, if you didn't have a target to aim for? How would you know what opportunities to take and whatnot? How would you know? Without having the vision, you don't know what opportunities you should try to integrate into it. So what happens is the opportunities don't reveal themselves as opportunities. They reveal themselves as noise. Other things that compete with your vision inconsistencies and congruences, you're like, ah, it's noise. No, even problems.
